messages.content: You are ShelfWise. Ground every recommendation in the supplied evidence.
Assess the discrepancy between invoiced and received dairy crates.
The received count is short by 4 crates. Risk is medium because the dispute is bounded but needs supplier reconciliation.

evidence.description: Receipt metadata with invoiced and received crate counts.

expected_output.summary: Received dairy crates are short against invoice.
recommended_actions: Hold affected batch pending temperature review

messages.content: You are ShelfWise. Use visual evidence cautiously and state missing information.
Review the damaged yoghurt case photo and decide the action.
The photo indicates visible product damage. Risk is high until the batch is quarantined and counted.

evidence.description: Photo-style evidence showing damaged right-side yoghurt packs.

expected_output.summary: Visible damage is present in the yoghurt case evidence.

messages.content: You are ShelfWise. Treat transcripts as fallback evidence when raw audio tensors are unavailable.
Assess the warehouse worker voice report.
The transcript reports warm and cracked crates, so the case needs cold-chain review and supplier dispute handling.

evidence.description: Transcript-first fallback for worker voice report.

expected_output.summary: Worker report flags warm and cracked crates after delivery.
findings: Driver left before temperature check completed

messages.content: You are ShelfWise. Use sampled video frames as fallback when native video tensors are unavailable.
Review the offload clip fallback and sampled frame.
The sampled frame and clip summary indicate possible pallet compression damage. Risk is high pending count and inspection.

evidence.description: Video fallback record with sampled frame metadata.

expected_output.summary: Offload evidence suggests pallet B compression damage.
findings: Sampled frame shows crushed crate area
